title: Tutkimus tukee metsiä koskevaa päätöksentekoa
content:
Research helps make better decisions about forests

heheiki@helsinki.fi Tue, 12/05/2026 - 12:45
Professor of Forest Planning Kyle Eyvindson's research aims to support forestry-related decision-making that are informed, transparent, and aligned with both forest owners and societal needs. He is inspired by the rapid growth in forest data and the new possibilities it opens for smarter, more transparent planning.

What are your research topics?

title: Juhani Kivimäki väittelee aiheesta Luottamusarvoihin perustuva suorituskyvyn estimointi koneoppimismallien monitoroinnissa
content:
Juhani Kivimäki defends his PhD thesis on Confidence-based Performance Estimation in Machine Learning Model Monitoring

ronkaine@helsinki.fi Tue, 12/05/2026 - 09:58
On Friday the 22nd of May 2026, M.Sc. Juhani Kivimäki defends his PhD thesis on Confidence-based Performance Estimation in Machine Learning Model Monitoring. The thesis is related to research done in the Department of Computer Science and in the Empirical Software Engineering group.

title: Single radioactive cloud caused Fukushima particle contamination
content:
Single radioactive cloud caused Fukushima particle contamination

merimaaj@helsinki.fi Mon, 11/05/2026 - 16:31
A single radioactive cloud was likely responsible for dispersing highly radioactive microscopic particles across large parts of Fukushima Prefecture during the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster. The finding helps us understand the dispersion of radioactive materials after the accident.

A

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.
